./fsm/utils/os_utils.pyr	
ProcessExecutionError.__init__c
Nz!Unexpected error while running %s
-z&%s

DEBUGr
messager
__str__(
ProcessExecutionError.__str__)
NNNNN)
__name__
__module__
__qualname__r	
__classcell__r
sJd |
d'd(
Helper method to shell out and execute a command through subprocess.
    :param cmd:             Passed to subprocess.Popen.
    :type cmd:              string
    :param process_input:   Send to opened process.
    :type process_input:    string
    :param shell:           whether or not there should be a shell used to
                            execute this command. Defaults to false.
    :type shell:            boolean
    :param check_exit_code: Single bool, int, or list of allowed exit
                            codes.  Defaults to [0].  Raise
                            :class:`ProcessExecutionError` unless
                            program exits with one of these code.
    :type check_exit_code:  boolean, int, or [int]
    :param attempts:        How many times to retry cmd.
    :type attempts:         int
    :param delay_on_retry:  True | False. Defaults to True. If set to True,
                            wait a short amount of time before retrying.
    :type delay_on_retry:   boolean
    :param interval:        The multiplier defined in seconds. it is valid
                            with backoff_rate parameter to controll exponential
                            backoff retries
    :type interval:         int
    :param backoff_rate:    Used for the exponential backoff retries replaces
                            random sleep times. Disabled if delay_on_retry is
                            passed as a parameter.
    :type backoff_rate:     int
    :param timeout:         Timeout defined in seconds. To use the timeout
                            mechanism to stop the subprocess with a specific
                            signal
    :type timeout:          int
    :param signal:          Signal to use to stop the process on timeout
    :param raise_timeout:   Raise an exception on timeout. Defaults to raise
    :type raise_timeout:            boolean
    :returns:               Tuple with stdout and stderr
    )
zdTimeout mechanism is controlled with timeout, signal,
        and raise_timeout parameters.
        z8Stopping %(cmd)s with signal %(signal)s after %(time)ss.)
signalr
time
warningZ
send_signal)
proc)

Track process creation asynchronously.
        It will be called upon process creation with
        the Popen object as a argument.
        r

        It will be called upon process completion with the
        Popen object as a argument.

msgr
execute_cmd@
Retrying an action to handle potential failures.
    :param exception:    Retry on certain exception.
    :param interval:     The multiplier defined in seconds. it is valid
                         with backoff_rate parameter to controll exponential
                         backoff retries.
    :param retries:      A number of tentative.
    :backoff_rate:       Exponential backoff retries.
    :max_interval:       Max interval in seconds beyond which the interval
                         will not be incremented. If it is set, it will be
                         retried indefinitely until an exception is thrown.
    :catch_exception:    Raise any exceptions or not.
    c
exc)
	exceptionr
_retry_on_exception
z"retry.<locals>._retry_on_exceptionc

minr'

z4t	
Run a command through SSH.
    :param ip:              Remote node ip address.
    :param cmd:             The command string to run.
    :param check_exit_code: If an exception should be raised for non-zero
                            exit.
    :param retry:           Max times to retry connecting.
    :param user:            User name for SSH login that free password
    :returns:               (stdout, stderr) from command execution through
                            SSH.
    rE
/etc/cvk/user_info.conf
permit_user_sshr&
Nzdtimeout 15 ssh -q -o ServerAliveInterval=5 -o ServerAliveCountMax=2 -o ConnectionAttempts=2 %s@%s %sT)

Run a command in cluster through SSH.
    :param local_ip:        local node ip in cluster.
    :param node_ips:        All ip of the cluster node.
    :param cmd:             The command string to run.
    :param check_exit_code: If an exception should be raised for exit_status
                            not in check_exit_code.
    :ignore_error:          If true we ignore execute exception and continue,
                            otherwise we stop and return.
    :ssh_test               If true test ssh connection before execute command,
                            otherwise ignore test.
    :returns:               Dictionary of command execution result through SSH.
    r6
ignore_errorF
ssh_testr_
Z	ssh_error
unamez
Ssh to %s test failNrE
zEIgnore error because ip type is different. Localip: %s, Remote ip: %s)
dictrN
SSHConnectTestFailedr
append)
local_ipZ
node_ipsr
node_ipr^
ssh_execute_cmd_on_nodesW
Unlink system links.
    By default it will raise an exception if a link does not exist and stop
    unlinking remaining links.
    This behavior can be modified passing optional parameters `no_errors` and
    `raise_at_end`.
    :param no_errors: Don't raise an exception on error
    :param raise_at_end: Don't raise an exception on first error, try to
                         unlink all links and then raise a ChainedException
                         with all the errors that where found.
    
	no_errorsF
raise_at_endz
Unlink failed for %sN)
getr
ExceptionChainer
contextry

clear_path
Trace calls to the decorated function.
    This decorator should always be defined as the outermost decorator so it
    is defined last. This is important so it does not interfere
    with other decorators.
    Using this decorator on a function will cause its execution to be logged at
    `DEBUG` level with arguments, return values, and exceptions.
    :returns: a function decorator
    c
==> %(func)s: call %(all_args)r)
all_argsi

z,<== %(func)s: return (%(time)dms) %(result)r)
